tool.name: codcmp
tool.summary: Compare codon usage between two coding-sequence or codon-profile sources

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `codcmp` compares normalized codon usage between two inputs and reports codon-by-codon count and frequency differences. In v1 both sides are normalized into the shared 61-sense-codon profile model before comparison.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts two local inputs. Each side may be either a strict coding-sequence file or a normalized codon-profile TSV produced by `codcopy`.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The result is a stable table report over the 61 sense codons. Each row records the codon, amino acid, left and right counts, left and right frequencies, and the frequency delta. The shared result summary also includes total variation distance across the compared profiles.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema codcmp`. Rust service coverage compares two committed coding fixtures and verifies representative codon count differences in the emitted table.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release is intentionally narrow and local-only. Coding inputs are subject to the same strict validation rules as the rest of the codon-analysis cohort, and only normalized TSV profiles with the governed header format are accepted as profile inputs.

artifacts.label: Left codon-reference FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed left-hand coding fixture with preferred leucine codons.

artifacts.label: Right codon-compare FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed right-hand coding fixture with rarer leucine codon usage for comparison.

examples.title: Compare codon usage between two committed coding fixtures
examples.description: Normalizes two committed coding FASTA fixtures and reports codon-by-codon differences plus aggregate distance.

expected_outputs.label: Codon comparison table
expected_outputs.description: A stable comparison table in which the `CTT` row shows `5` counts on the left and `0` counts on the right for the committed fixtures.
